note: Curated catalog of open, runnable sequence and single-cell foundation models relevant to rare cancer analysis. Every entry is grounded in a primary source (paper or model card). Model catalogs, checkpoints, and licenses evolve; verify against the live HuggingFace or GitHub page before use. This is a research reference, not medical advice.

models.name: Evo 2
models.footprint: 1B/7B tractable on a single GPU; 40B is multi-GPU. 1 Mb context.
models.rare_cancer_use: Zero-shot scoring of coding and noncoding variants in rare tumor drivers across a 1 Mb window.

models.name: Nucleotide Transformer
models.footprint: 50M to 500M variants run on a single GPU; 2.5B is heavier.
models.rare_cancer_use: Zero-shot variant effect (ClinVar AUC ~0.80, eQTL 0.73, meQTL 0.71) for interpreting variants of uncertain significance.
models.citation: Dalla-Torre et al., Nature Methods 2024, PMID 39609566, doi:10.1038/s41592-024-02523-z

models.name: DNABERT-2
models.footprint: Compact (~470 MB fp32); runs on a modest GPU or CPU.
models.rare_cancer_use: Lightweight genomic sequence embeddings around a locus for downstream classification.

models.name: Borzoi
models.footprint: 524 kb input at 32 bp bins; single GPU. Flashzoi is a fast variant.
models.rare_cancer_use: Predict how a noncoding or regulatory variant shifts RNA-seq coverage of a driver gene.
models.citation: Linder et al., Nature Genetics 2025, doi:10.1038/s41588-024-02053-6

models.name: Enformer
models.rare_cancer_use: Sequence-to-expression reference model for regulatory variant effects near driver loci.
models.citation: Avsec et al., Nature Methods 2021, doi:10.1038/s41592-021-01252-x

models.name: SpliceAI
models.rare_cancer_use: Flag splice-disruptive variants in driver genes (top performer alongside Pangolin in independent benchmarks).

models.name: Pangolin
models.rare_cancer_use: Quantitative splice-site usage change in four tissues; strong on splice-disruptive variant calling.
models.citation: Zeng and Li, Genome Biology 2022, doi:10.1186/s13059-022-02664-4

models.name: ESM-2
models.footprint: 8M to 650M run comfortably on a single GPU or CPU; 15B is multi-GPU.
models.rare_cancer_use: Zero-shot masked-marginal scoring of missense variants in rare tumor drivers (SMARCB1, VHL, BAP1, SF3B1); per-residue constraint maps.
models.citation: Lin et al., Science 2023, doi:10.1126/science.ade2574; ESM1v scoring: Meier et al., NeurIPS 2021

models.name: ESMFold
models.footprint: GPU with ample memory; single-sequence, no MSA.
models.rare_cancer_use: MSA-free 3D structure of a mutant driver to inspect where a missense variant falls, an order of magnitude faster than AlphaFold2.

models.name: AlphaMissense
models.footprint: None; precomputed scores are a download.
models.rare_cancer_use: Reclassify missense VUS in rare tumor drivers; validated on BAP1 in uveal melanoma. Reported ~92% sensitivity, ~78% specificity vs ACMG.
models.caveat: Precomputed scores are non-commercial; performance on clinical databases does not always generalize to real cohorts (see PrimateAI-3D).

models.name: PrimateAI-3D
models.rare_cancer_use: Missense effect predictor that outperformed AlphaMissense on some rare-disease de novo cohorts and biobank benchmarks.

models.name: scGPT
models.footprint: GPU recommended (flash-attn optional); CPU possible but slow.
models.rare_cancer_use: Embed rare-tumor transcriptomes with the pan-cancer checkpoint; annotate and integrate cells.
models.citation: Cui et al., Nature Methods 2024, doi:10.1038/s41592-024-02201-0
models.caveat: Zero-shot performance does not consistently beat HVG/scVI/Harmony baselines; benchmark and consider fine-tuning.

models.name: Geneformer
models.rare_cancer_use: Rank-value transcriptome embedding and in-silico perturbation of rare-tumor cell states.
models.caveat: Zero-shot performance does not consistently beat simple baselines; benchmark and consider fine-tuning.

models.name: UCE (Universal Cell Embeddings)
models.footprint: 4-layer runs on a modest GPU; 33-layer is heavier.
models.rare_cancer_use: Zero-shot embedding of a rare-tumor cell atlas into a shared space; outperformed scGPT/Geneformer on the standard embedding benchmark.

models.name: scFoundation
models.rare_cancer_use: Large read-depth-aware transcriptome model for embedding and imputation of rare-tumor scRNA-seq.
models.citation: Hao et al., Nature Methods 2024, doi:10.1038/s41592-024-02305-7
models.caveat: Included in single-cell benchmarks where foundation models do not consistently beat task-specific methods.

benchmark_caveat: Multiple independent 2025 to 2026 peer-reviewed benchmarks (Genome Biology 26:101; Advanced Science 2026 scEval; Nature Methods 2025) agree that single-cell foundation models do NOT reliably beat simple baselines (highly variable genes, scVI, Harmony, PCA) in zero-shot use. Any rare-tumor analysis relying on their zero-shot embeddings should benchmark against these simpler methods and consider fine-tuning. DNA and protein language models (ESM-2, Nucleotide Transformer, Evo 2) have stronger zero-shot variant-effect evidence.

tasks_glossary.variant_effect: Score how likely a sequence change is to be damaging.
tasks_glossary.missense_pathogenicity: Classify a protein-coding missense change as pathogenic or benign.
tasks_glossary.splice_disruption: Predict whether a variant breaks or creates a splice site.
tasks_glossary.expression_from_sequence: Predict gene expression or coverage tracks directly from DNA sequence.
tasks_glossary.protein_embedding: Represent a protein sequence as a vector for downstream tasks.
tasks_glossary.structure_prediction: Predict a 3D protein structure from sequence.
tasks_glossary.cell_type_annotation: Assign cell types to single-cell transcriptomes.
tasks_glossary.batch_integration: Remove technical batch effects across single-cell datasets.
tasks_glossary.perturbation: Predict transcriptional response to a genetic or chemical perturbation.
tasks_glossary.embedding: Map data into a learned representation space.
